descriptions.value: Apache Log4j2 JNDI features do not protect against attacker-controlled LDAP and other JNDI related endpoints. An attacker who can control log messages or log message parameters can execute arbitrary code loaded from LDAP servers. Known as Log4Shell.

It was found that the fix to address CVE-2021-44228 in Apache Log4j 2.15.0 was incomplete in certain non-default configurations. Attackers can craft malicious input data using a JNDI Lookup pattern resulting in a denial of service or information leak.
Apache Log4j2 versions 2.0-alpha1 through 2.16.0 did not protect from uncontrolled recursion from self-referential lookups, allowing attackers to cause DoS via crafted strings.

The BN_mod_sqrt() function in OpenSSL, which computes a modular square root, contains a bug that can cause it to loop forever for non-prime moduli. This can be triggered by malformed certificates, causing a denial of service.
The c_rehash script in OpenSSL does not properly sanitise shell metacharacters, allowing command injection with the privileges of the script.
Use-after-free following BIO_new_NDEF in OpenSSL. A attacker can cause memory corruption via crafted inputs, leading to denial of service or potential code execution.
A type confusion vulnerability in OpenSSL relating to X.400 address processing inside an X.509 GeneralName, allowing memory reads or denial of service.
Issue processing some specially crafted ASN.1 object identifiers or data in OpenSSL could cause excessive resource usage resulting in denial of service.
Processing a maliciously formatted PKCS12 file may lead OpenSSL to crash, causing a denial of service via a NULL pointer dereference.

The Heartbleed bug in OpenSSL 1.0.1 allows remote attackers to obtain sensitive information from process memory via crafted TLS heartbeat packets. This exposes private keys, user credentials, and session tokens.

When curl saves cookies, alt-svc and hsts data to local files, it makes the operation atomic by renaming a temporary file to the final target. This allows a local attacker to change the permissions of the final file.
curl before 7.86.0 has a double free vulnerability in the HTTP proxy connection code. An attacker controlling a malicious HTTP proxy can trigger this.
A heap-based buffer overflow in the SOCKS5 proxy handshake in curl can be triggered by a malicious SOCKS5 server sending an extremely long hostname, allowing remote code execution.
A use-after-free in curl's HTTP multi-request handling when combining POST and PUT requests can lead to information disclosure.
When an application tells libcurl it wants to allow HTTP/2 server push, and the server does push a header field that does not exist, libcurl has a memory leak that grows until the transfer ends.
An issue in the urllib.parse component of Python before 3.11.4 allows attackers to bypass blocklisting methods by supplying a URL with a blank host component.
An issue in CPython 3.11 through 3.11.0 allows denial of service via a very long idna name that triggers a quadratic time algorithm in the encodings.idna module.
The email module of Python incorrectly parses email addresses that contain a special character, allowing attackers to spoof email senders.
An issue in the zipfile module of CPython allows extraction of crafted zip archives to directories outside the intended destination tree via path traversal.
The HTTP/2 protocol allows a denial of service because request cancellation can reset many streams quickly. Known as the HTTP/2 Rapid Reset attack, exploited in large-scale DDoS campaigns.
NGINX Open Source and NGINX Plus have a vulnerability in the ngx_http_mp4_module, which might allow an attacker to corrupt NGINX worker memory via a specially crafted mp4 file.
NGINX Open Source and NGINX Plus have a vulnerability in the ngx_http_mp4_module, which might allow an attacker to read memory beyond the shared memory region.
Sudo before 1.9.5p2 contains a heap-based buffer overflow that allows privilege escalation to root via sudoedit -s. Known as Baron Samedit.
In Sudo before 1.8.28, an attacker with access to a Runas ALL sudoer account can bypass the restrictions by specifying the user ID -1 or 4294967295 to execute commands as root.
Sudo before 1.9.15 may be susceptible to a rowhammer-style attack that bypasses password or user restriction policies via sudo_debug calls.
The PKCS#11 feature in ssh-agent in OpenSSH before 9.3p2 has an insufficiently trustworthy search path, leading to remote code execution if an agent is forwarded to an attacker-controlled system.
A signal handler race condition in OpenSSH's server (sshd) allows unauthenticated remote code execution as root on glibc-based Linux systems. Regression of CVE-2006-5051. Known as regreSSHion.
In OpenSSH before 9.6, OS command injection via shell metacharacters is possible in ProxyCommand, ProxyJump, and related settings when hostnames contain special characters.
Git on multi-user machines where git is installed in a world-writable directory allows another user to place a hostile .git directory that affects operations run by the first user.
Git's submodule deinit may write a config value with a specially crafted name that overrides the user's git config, leading to privilege escalation or code execution on the next git invocation.
Git on case-insensitive filesystems with support for symlinks is vulnerable to remote code execution during clone operations via a symlink pointing to the .git directory.

GNU Bash through 4.3 processes trailing strings after function definitions in environment variable values, allowing remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted environment. Known as Shellshock.
An issue in GNU Bash allows a privileged user to bypass restrictions when they are set via the PATH_RESTRICTED option, leading to privilege escalation.
The iconv() function in the GNU C Library has a buffer overflow when converting strings to the ISO-2022-CN-EXT character set, allowing heap-based buffer overflow and potential code execution.
A flaw in glibc's getaddrinfo() function allows an attacker to trigger a memory leak via crafted hostname inputs, potentially causing a denial of service.
Expat (libexpat) through 2.4.4 has a signed integer overflow in XML_GetBuffer, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service or potential code execution.
libexpat before 2.4.9 has a use-after-free in the doContent function in xmlparse.c, which can be triggered by malformed XML input.
libexpat through 2.5.0 allows denial of service via stack exhaustion when processing large tokens in deeply nested XML entities.
SQLite 1.0.12 through 3.39.x before 3.39.2 sometimes allows an array-bounds overflow when a string argument is passed to a printf function.
A vulnerability was found in SQLite where the sessionReadRecord function in ext/session/sqlite3session.c can be exploited via a crafted database file to cause a use-after-free.
zlib through 1.2.12 has a heap-based buffer over-read or buffer overflow in inflate() via a large gzip header extra field, triggered by calling inflateGetHeader.
An integer overflow leading to an out-of-bounds write was found in libxml2 when parsing XML content with large entity names, allowing denial of service or code execution.
An issue in libxml2 allows a use-after-free in xmlDictGrow if entity URIs with unusual character sequences are processed, corrupting the internal dictionary.
FreeType commit 1e2eb65048f75c64b68708efed6ce904c31f3b2f allows remote attackers to cause a heap buffer overflow via a crafted TTF font file.
FreeType commit 53dfdcd8198d2b3201a23c4bad9190519ba918db allows remote attackers to cause a NULL pointer dereference leading to denial of service via a crafted font file.

An out-of-bounds read vulnerability was discovered in the PCRE2 library in the compile_xclass_matchingpath() function, allowing an attacker to disclose information or cause denial of service via crafted regex patterns.
An out-of-bounds read vulnerability was discovered in the PCRE2 library in the get_recurse_data_length() function of pcre2_jit_compile.c, which can be triggered by crafted regex patterns.

An arbitrary file write vulnerability was found in GNU gzip's zgrep utility. When zgrep is applied to a file with a specially crafted name, an attacker can overwrite an arbitrary file writable by the user.
GNU tar through 1.34 has a one-byte out-of-bounds read that results in use of uninitialized memory for a conditional jump, triggered by a V7 archive via oldgnu_header in list.c and xheader.c.
BZ2_decompress in decompress.c in bzip2 through 1.0.6 has an out-of-bounds write when there are many selectors, allowing remote attackers to cause denial of service or potential code execution.

Malicious code was discovered in upstream tarballs of xz-utils versions 5.6.0 and 5.6.1. The backdoor manipulates sshd via systemd, allowing a remote attacker with a specific key to execute arbitrary commands as root.
ncurses before 6.4-20230408 has memory-corruption issues when processing malformed terminfo data, allowing stack or heap corruption via crafted terminfo data.
There is a stack overflow vulnerability in ash.c in BusyBox through 1.35.0 via a crafted shell script that can lead to arbitrary code execution.
A use-after-free vulnerability in the awk applet in BusyBox allows attackers to cause denial of service or potentially execute arbitrary code via crafted awk input.
Heap buffer overflow in libwebp allows a remote attacker to perform an out of bounds memory write via a crafted WebP image, affecting any software embedding libwebp including browsers.
runc through 1.1.11 has an internal file descriptor leak that allows a specially crafted container image to escape the container's root filesystem, gaining access to the host. Known as Leaky Vessels.
containerd OCI image importer does not properly validate the target path of extracted archives, allowing a path traversal attack to write files outside the expected container root.
containerd allows attackers to bypass supplementary group permissions by crafting container images that exploit incorrect handling of supplementary groups in the OCI runtime.
A Spring MVC or Spring WebFlux application running on JDK 9+ may be vulnerable to remote code execution via data binding. Known as Spring4Shell. Requires JDK 9+, Apache Tomcat, and WAR deployment.

In Spring Cloud Function before 3.1.7 and 3.2.3, when using routing functionality, it is possible for a user to provide a specially crafted SpEL as a routing-expression that may result in remote code execution.

Spring Framework 6.0.0-6.0.6 and 5.3.0-5.3.25 are vulnerable to a security bypass when using SecurityAnnotationMethodInterceptor and Spring Security's method security, allowing access control to be bypassed.

Apache Commons Text performs variable interpolation, allowing properties lookup. If certain interpolators such as script, dns, or url are enabled, an attacker can trigger remote code execution. Known as Text4Shell.
In FasterXML jackson-databind, resource exhaustion can occur because of a lack of a check in primitive value deserializers to avoid deep wrapper array nesting, allowing denial of service attacks.
In FasterXML jackson-databind before 2.13.4, use of deeply nested arrays with UNWRAP_SINGLE_VALUE_ARRAYS in combination with a large number of values causes a stack overflow.
The fix for CVE-2023-24998 in Apache Tomcat was incomplete. An attacker can trigger unlimited number of file parts in a multipart request, causing a denial of service.
Improper input validation in Apache Tomcat allows an attacker to perform HTTP request smuggling by sending crafted HTTP/1.1 requests with malformed content-length headers.
Redis before 6.0.19, 6.2.x before 6.2.12, and 7.0.x before 7.0.11 is vulnerable to a denial of service via the LMPOP command with an integer overflow in the count argument.
Redis before 6.2.7 and 7.0 contains a vulnerability where a malicious user can cause a denial of service by providing a crafted Lua script that causes the Lua interpreter to crash.
PostgreSQL's row security policy can be bypassed via certain SET ROLE sequences, allowing users to read or modify data they are not supposed to access.
Late privilege drop in REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY in PostgreSQL allows an object creator to execute arbitrary SQL functions as the materialized view owner.
A malicious HTTP/2 client which rapidly creates requests and immediately resets them can cause excessive server resource consumption in the Go HTTP/2 server, similar to the HTTP/2 Rapid Reset attack.
Requests (Python) before 2.31.0 forwards proxy-authorization headers to destination servers when encountering cross-origin redirects, potentially leaking credentials.
Pillow before 10.0.0 allows uncontrolled resource consumption via crafted ImageFont inputs using PIL.ImageFont.ImageFont, when the font exceeds a threshold size, leading to denial of service.
Pillow before 9.3.0 allows an image bomb via decompression when opening a crafted file, leading to excessive memory usage and denial of service.
cryptography before 41.0.6 allows NULL pointer dereference when loading PKCS12 files via the rust-openssl bindings when certain optional fields are absent, causing denial of service.
cryptography before 42.0.4 allows a NULL pointer dereference when serializing an PKCS12 object with an invalid encryption algorithm OID, potentially causing denial of service.
A vulnerability was discovered in PyYAML where the yaml.load() function allows remote code execution via unsafe deserialization of Python objects, when the default Loader is used.
Werkzeug multipart data parser is vulnerable to denial of service through resource exhaustion when processing carefully crafted multipart form data.
Python Packaging Authority setuptools before 65.5.1 has a regular expression denial of service (ReDoS) vulnerability in package_index.py when processing HTML pages from package indexes.
Paramiko before 3.4.0 is affected by the Terrapin attack, which allows a man-in-the-middle attacker to downgrade the security of an SSH connection by truncating the handshake extension negotiation.
aiohttp before 3.9.2 has a path traversal vulnerability in its static file serving functionality, allowing an attacker to access files outside the intended directory via URL manipulation.

In Django 3.2 before 3.2.19, 4.x before 4.1.9, and 4.2 before 4.2.1, EmailValidator and URLValidator are subject to a potential ReDoS (regular expression denial of service) attack via crafted email and URL inputs.

Django before 4.2.11 and 5.0.x before 5.0.3 is vulnerable to a potential denial of service in django.utils.text.Truncator.words() and chars() when truncating HTML content with very long strings without whitespace.
Flask before 2.3.2 and 2.2.x before 2.2.5 may allow clients to bypass same-site cookie restrictions. If the application uses session cookies, the session may be accessible to third-party sites.
A parsing vulnerability for the MessageSet type in the ProtocolBuffers library can lead to out of memory failures. A specially crafted proto payload can trigger the issue.
GNU Wget before 1.21.2 does not restrict the Authorization header on cross-origin redirects, potentially exposing authentication credentials to unauthorized third-party servers.
ImageMagick before 7.1.1-1 has undefined behavior (for example, division by zero and heap-based buffer over-read) in the ReadRLEImage function of coders/rle.c.
ImageMagick 7.1.0-49 is vulnerable to denial of service. When it parses a PNG image (e.g. for resize), the convert process could be left waiting for stdin input.
